questions.question: Explain the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its importance in e-waste management.
keyPoints: Definition of EPR: producers take responsibility for collection/recycling at end-of-life
Reduces burden on municipalities and environment

questions.question: List any four hazardous substances present in e-waste and explain one health impact of each.
Describe the 3R principle of waste management with reference to e-waste.
keyPoints: Reduce: minimize generation through conscious purchasing, longer product life
Reuse: use products again for same/different purpose (donate, resell)
Recycle: process materials for new products
Hierarchy: Reduce > Reuse > Recycle in terms of preference

questions.question: What is 'Urban Mining' in the context of e-waste management? Explain its significance.
keyPoints: Urban mining = recovering valuable metals from e-waste
Significance: reduces need for traditional mining
Precious metals: gold, silver, copper, palladium

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in the e-waste recycling process.
keyPoints: Collection: gathering e-waste from various sources
Sorting: categorizing by type and condition
Refining: chemical processes to extract pure materials
Disposal: safe handling of residual waste

questions.question: Discuss the environmental impacts of improper e-waste disposal.
Explain the E-Waste Management Rules 2016 in India. Discuss the roles and responsibilities of producers, consumers, and recyclers under these rules.
keyPoints: Producer responsibilities: EPR registration, collection targets, financing
Consumer responsibilities: proper disposal at authorized collection points
Recycler responsibilities: authorization, safe processing, reporting
Role of CPCB and SPCB in implementation

questions.question: Describe the various methods of e-waste disposal and their environmental impacts. Also suggest measures for proper e-waste management.
keyPoints: Methods: Landfilling, Incineration, Open burning, Informal recycling
Recommended measures: proper collection, recycling, regulations

questions.question: Explain the Basel Convention and its relevance to e-waste management. Also discuss the challenges in implementing e-waste regulations in developing countries.
keyPoints: Basel Convention: international treaty on hazardous waste transfer
Key provisions: prior informed consent, ban on exports to developing countries
Challenges: lack of infrastructure, informal sector, awareness, enforcement
Solutions: capacity building, formalization of recyclers, international cooperation

questions.question: Discuss the health hazards associated with improper handling of e-waste. What safety measures should be adopted by workers in e-waste recycling facilities?
keyPoints: Health hazards: lead poisoning, mercury toxicity, cadmium effects
Respiratory problems, neurological damage, birth defects
Safety measures: protective equipment, ventilation, training
